The custom bags were available to purchase on [Amazon.com](/wiki/Amazon.com "Amazon.com"), with Doritos also hosting Rock Out Loud pop-up recording booths in New York and Los Angeles where fans could sing the songs from *Awesome Mix Vol. 2* and have the opportunity to win various prizes, including the custom bags, concert tickets, and free bags of Doritos."[[175]](#cite_note-DoritosBag-177)

In July 2016, Gunn, Pratt and other members of the cast attended San Diego Comic-Con to promote the film,[[176]](#cite_note-SDCC2016-178) showing footage and revealing additional character information.[[34]](#cite_note-SDCC2016CBR-36) On October 19, a "sneak peek" teaser was released ahead of the first full teaser trailer. Ethan Anderton of [/Film](/wiki//Film "/Film") felt the teaser was strong, despite not showing any of the new characters or relying on Baby Groot,[[177]](#cite_note-/FilmTeaser-179) while *[The A.V. Club](/wiki/The_A.V._Club "The A.V. Club")*'s Esther Zuckerman called it "an immediate crowd-pleaser".[[178]](#cite_note-AVClubTeaser-180) According to media-measurement firm [Comscore](/wiki/Comscore "Comscore") and its PreAct service, the teaser was the top trailer for the week it released, generating 108,000 new social media conversations.[[179]](#cite_note-VarietyTeaserTop-181) In early December, before Gunn revealed the first teaser trailer at [Comic Con Experience](/wiki/Comic_Con_Experience "Comic Con Experience") 2016,[[180]](#cite_note-TrailerCCXP-182)[[181]](#cite_note-TrailerACCDebut-183) he said that finding scenes and moments to showcase in the trailer without revealing too much of the film resulted in hard choices, since "people really go through every single little shot and try to figure out what the movie's about. And there's a lot of mysteries in *Guardians 2*."[[182]](#cite_note-FirstTeaserDone-184) Describing the teaser trailer, Jacob Hall of /Film noted that the trailer was able to succeed without giving too much away as it focused on character interactions rather than plot.[[183]](#cite_note-/FilmFirstTrailer-185) The teaser trailer received 81 million views in 24 hours, becoming the second-most viewed teaser behind *[Beauty and the Beast](/wiki/Beauty_and_the_Beast_(2017_film) "Beauty and the Beast (2017 film)")* and largest Marvel Studios teaser ever. Additionally, [Sweet](/wiki/Sweet_(band) "Sweet (band)")'s "[Fox on the Run](/wiki/Fox_on_the_Run_(Sweet_song) "Fox on the Run (Sweet song)")" reached number one on the [iTunes](/wiki/ITunes "ITunes") Rock Chart after featuring in the teaser.[[184]](#cite_note-FirstTrailerViews-186)

A second trailer aired during [Super Bowl LI](/wiki/Super_Bowl_LI "Super Bowl LI"). Germain Lussier for [io9](/wiki/Io9 "Io9") called it "hilarious",[[185]](#cite_note-SuperBowlio9-187) while Anderton said it was "one hell of a" Super Bowl spot, "one that probably overshadows the game itself for people like me... There's plenty of badass cosmic action, the humor we all love, a stellar soundtrack, and some great new footage from the sequel."[[186]](#cite_note-SuperBowl/Film-188) The spot generated the most Twitter conversation volume during the game with 47,800 conversations, according to Comscore, who measured the volume of trailers that aired during the game from the time it aired through the end of the game. The film also topped a [Fandango](/wiki/Fandango_Media "Fandango Media") survey of fans' favorite film trailer during the Super Bowl.[[187]](#cite_note-SuperBowlSpotData-189) An additional trailer debuted on February 28, 2017, on *Jimmy Kimmel Live!*. Haleigh Foutch at [Collider](/wiki/Collider_(website) "Collider (website)") felt the trailer added "hype" to the film, and was "just an all-around wonderful trailer, lit up with the wacky humor and irreverent charm that made the first film such a hit, with an extra dose of visual splendor."[[188]](#cite_note-FebTrailerCollider-190) Pratt and Saldaña appeared at the [2017 Kids' Choice Awards](/wiki/2017_Kids%27_Choice_Awards "2017 Kids' Choice Awards"), where they debuted an exclusive clip.[[189]](#cite_note-2017KidsChoiceAwards-191) In mid-July, Marvel created a 1980s-inspired [infomercial](/wiki/Infomercial "Infomercial") and an [800 number](/wiki/Toll-free_telephone_number "Toll-free telephone number") to promote the film's home media release.[[190]](#cite_note-HomeMediaInfomercial-192)

## Release

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Guardians_of_the_Galaxy_Vol._2&action=edit&section=12 "Edit section: Release")]

### Theatrical

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Guardians_of_the_Galaxy_Vol._2&action=edit&section=13 "Edit section: Theatrical")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/f/fa/Guardians_of_the_Galaxy_Vol._2_premiere_1.jpg/250px-Guardians_of_the_Galaxy_Vol._2_premiere_1.jpg)](/wiki/File:Guardians_of_the_Galaxy_Vol._2_premiere_1.jpg)

Premiere of *Guardians of the Galaxy Vol. 2* at the [Dolby Theatre](/wiki/Dolby_Theatre "Dolby Theatre") in [Hollywood, Los Angeles](/wiki/Hollywood,_Los_Angeles "Hollywood, Los Angeles")

*Guardians of the Galaxy Vol. 2* made its world premiere in Tokyo on April 10, 2017,[[191]](#cite_note-TokyoPremiere-193) and its [Hollywood, Los Angeles](/wiki/Hollywood,_Los_Angeles "Hollywood, Los Angeles"), premiere on April 19 at the [Dolby Theatre](/wiki/Dolby_Theatre "Dolby Theatre").[[192]](#cite_note-HollywoodPremiere-194) The film began its international release on April 25, in Australia, New Zealand, and Italy,[[193]](#cite_note-DeadlineIntWknd1-195) alongside a total of 37 markets in its first weekend, with 176 IMAX screens in 35 of those markets.[[194]](#cite_note-DeadlineIntWknd1New-196) Its North American release on May 5 took place in 4,347 theaters, of which over 3,800 were in 3D,[[195]](#cite_note-DeadlineWknd1-197) 388 in IMAX and [IMAX 3D](/wiki/IMAX_3D "IMAX 3D"),[[195]](#cite_note-DeadlineWknd1-197)[[196]](#cite_note-IMAX-198) 588 premium large-format, and 194 [D-Box](/wiki/D-Box_Technologies "D-Box Technologies") locations.[[195]](#cite_note-DeadlineWknd1-197) The film's opening in China was in 400 IMAX theaters, the largest ever for the country.[[197]](#cite_note-DeadlineIntWknd2-199) On May 4, 2017, 550 theaters in the United States had a special [RealD](/wiki/RealD "RealD") *Guardians of the Galaxy* double feature event before preview screenings of *Vol. 2*. Guests who attended received an exclusive mini poster and a set of souvenir collectible buttons.[[198]](#cite_note-DoubleFeatureScreening-200) *Vol. 2* was originally intended to be released on July 28, 2017.[[89]](#cite_note-GotG2Release-91) The film is part of Phase Three of the MCU.[[199]](#cite_note-PhaseThree-201)

### Home media

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Guardians_of_the_Galaxy_Vol._2&action=edit&section=14 "Edit section: Home media")]

*Guardians of the Galaxy Vol. 2* was released on [digital download](/wiki/Digital_distribution "Digital distribution") by [Walt Disney Studios Home Entertainment](/wiki/Walt_Disney_Studios_Home_Entertainment "Walt Disney Studios Home Entertainment") on August 8, 2017, and on [Blu-ray](/wiki/Blu-ray "Blu-ray"), Blu-ray 3D, [Ultra HD Blu-ray](/wiki/Ultra_HD_Blu-ray "Ultra HD Blu-ray"), and [DVD](/wiki/DVD-Video "DVD-Video") on August 22.[[200]](#cite_note-HomeMedia-202)[[201]](#cite_note-HomeMedia3D-203) The Ultra HD Blu-ray version is the first Disney home media release in [4K resolution](/wiki/4K_resolution "4K resolution").[[202]](#cite_note-4KUltraBluray-204) The digital and Blu-ray releases include behind-the-scenes featurettes, audio commentary, deleted scenes, a blooper reel, and a music video for the song "Guardians Inferno". The digital release also exclusively features the breakdown of three scenes, from their initial ideas to their completed versions, and a behind-the-scenes look at the [Guardians of the Galaxy – Mission: Breakout!](/wiki/Guardians_of_the_Galaxy_%E2%80%93_Mission:_Breakout! "Guardians of the Galaxy – Mission: Breakout!"), an accelerated [drop tower](/wiki/Drop_tower "Drop tower") [dark ride](/wiki/Dark_ride "Dark ride") attraction at [Disney California Adventure](/wiki/Disney_California_Adventure "Disney California Adventure").[[200]](#cite_note-HomeMedia-202) The 1970s-style music video for "Guardians Inferno" was directed by David Yarovesky and features Hasselhoff alongside James Gunn, Pratt, Saldaña, Bautista, Klementieff, Gillan, Rooker, and Sean Gunn. Stan Lee and [Guillermo Rodriguez](/wiki/Guillermo_Rodriguez_(comedian) "Guillermo Rodriguez (comedian)") also make cameo appearances in the video.[[203]](#cite_note-InfernoVideo-205)[[204]](#cite_note-InfernoVideo2-206)

The digital release of the film had the most digital downloads and largest opening week of any Marvel Studios film.[[205]](#cite_note-BiggestDigitalOpeningWeek-207) The physical releases in its first week of sale were the top home media release, selling "nearly three times as many discs as the rest of the top 10 sellers combined", according to [NPD VideoScan](/wiki/The_NPD_Group "The NPD Group") data. The Blu-ray version accounted for 83% of the sales, with 10% of total sales coming from the Ultra HD Blu-ray version.[[206]](#cite_note-HomeMediaSalesWk1-208) In its second week, the film was once again the top home media release.[[207]](#cite_note-HomeMediaSalesWk2-209) As well, total sales of *Vol. 2* in the United Kingdom were more than the other films in the top 40 combined; it was also the top film in the country.[[208]](#cite_note-UKHomeMediaSales-210) The IMAX Enhanced version of the film was made available on [Disney+](/wiki/Disney%2B "Disney+") beginning on November 12, 2021.[[209]](#cite_note-IMAXEnhanced-211)

## Reception

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Guardians_of_the_Galaxy_Vol._2&action=edit&section=15 "Edit section: Reception")]

### Box office

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Guardians_of_the_Galaxy_Vol._2&action=edit&section=16 "Edit section: Box office")]

*Guardians of the Galaxy Vol. 2* grossed over $389.8 million in the United States and Canada, and over $473.9 million in other countries, for a worldwide total of $863.8 million.[[2]](#cite_note-BOM-2) The film had earlier surpassed the first film's gross ($773 million) by [Memorial Day](/wiki/Memorial_Day "Memorial Day") weekend, three weeks after release, with $783.3 million worldwide,[[210]](#cite_note-DeadlineIntWknd5-212) and became the fifth-highest-grossing MCU film a week later.[[211]](#cite_note-DeadlineIntWknd6-213) *[Deadline Hollywood](/wiki/Deadline_Hollywood "Deadline Hollywood")* calculated the film's net profit as $157 million, accounting for production budgets, marketing, talent participations, and other costs; box office grosses and home media revenues placed it ninth on their list of 2017's "Most Valuable Blockbusters".[[212]](#cite_note-DeadlineProfit-214)

Since tickets went on sale on April 24, 2017, the film was the number one seller on [Fandango](/wiki/Fandango_(ticket_service) "Fandango (ticket service)"), and surpassed the advance sales of *[Avengers: Age of Ultron](/wiki/Avengers:_Age_of_Ultron "Avengers: Age of Ultron")* (2015) in the similar time frame.[[213]](#cite_note-FandangoPreSale-215) Over 80% of sales on MovieTickets.com were for the film ahead of its release.[[195]](#cite_note-DeadlineWknd1-197) *Vol. 2* earned $146.5 million in its opening weekend in the United States and Canada,[[214]](#cite_note-DeadlineWknd1Final-216) with IMAX contributing $13 million.[[197]](#cite_note-DeadlineIntWknd2-199) The $17 million that came from Thursday night previews was the highest preview amount of 2017.[[195]](#cite_note-DeadlineWknd1-197) The film had previously been projected to earn upwards of $160 million in its opening weekend,[[198]](#cite_note-DoubleFeatureScreening-200)[[215]](#cite_note-DeadlineBOIntWk1Preview-217) with *Deadline Hollywood* predicting it could reach the $179 million debut of *Captain America: Civil War*.[[216]](#cite_note-DeadlineBOApril2017-218) It remained at number one in its second weekend,[[217]](#cite_note-DeadlineWknd2-219) and fell to second in its third, behind *[Alien: Covenant](/wiki/Alien:_Covenant "Alien: Covenant")*.[[218]](#cite_note-DeadlineWknd3-220) *Vol. 2* remained at number two in its fourth weekend, this time behind *[Pirates of the Caribbean: Dead Men Tell No Tales](/wiki/Pirates_of_the_Caribbean:_Dead_Men_Tell_No_Tales "Pirates of the Caribbean: Dead Men Tell No Tales")*, and crossed $337.6 million, surpassing the domestic gross of the first film.[[219]](#cite_note-DeadlineWknd4-221) By its fifth weekend, the film fell to fourth,[[220]](#cite_note-DeadlineWknd5-222) and in its next, fell to fifth.[[221]](#cite_note-DeadlineWknd6-223) *Vol. 2* continued to remain in the top 10 for two more weeks, placing ninth in its seventh weekend,[[222]](#cite_note-DeadlineWknd7-224) and tenth in its eighth weekend.[[223]](#cite_note-DeadlineWknd8-225)

Outside of the United States and Canada, the film earned $106 million in its first weekend from 37 markets, becoming the top film in them all except Portugal, Turkey, and Vietnam. IMAX contributed $5 million to the opening-weekend gross. The film also outperformed the original's opening weekend in all markets except Belgium.[[194]](#cite_note-DeadlineIntWknd1New-196) In its second weekend, the sequel opened as the top film in South Korea, China, and Russia.[[197]](#cite_note-DeadlineIntWknd2-199) It had the highest opening for an MCU film in Austria,[[194]](#cite_note-DeadlineIntWknd1New-196) the second-highest in Australia ($11.8 million), the Netherlands ($500,000),[[193]](#cite_note-DeadlineIntWknd1-195)[[194]](#cite_note-DeadlineIntWknd1New-196) Germany ($9.3 million), and the United Kingdom ($16.9 million),[[194]](#cite_note-DeadlineIntWknd1New-196) and the third-highest in New Zealand ($400,000), Italy ($1.4 million),[[193]](#cite_note-DeadlineIntWknd1-195) and Russia ($11.6 million).[[197]](#cite_note-DeadlineIntWknd2-199) The New Zealand and Netherlands openings were also the highest of 2017 for the countries,[[193]](#cite_note-DeadlineIntWknd1-195) while Germany and the United Kingdom's were the second-highest.[[193]](#cite_note-DeadlineIntWknd1-195)[[194]](#cite_note-DeadlineIntWknd1New-196) In South Korea, it had the biggest opening day ($3.3 million) and second-best opening weekend ($13.3 million) of 2017, the latter surpassing the original's entire earnings in the country. It also had the biggest May opening day and the third-highest opening day for an MCU film there.[[197]](#cite_note-DeadlineIntWknd2-199)[[224]](#cite_note-DeadlineIntMidWknd1-2-226) Ukraine had the second-largest opening ever, while in Puerto Rico, the film had the largest IMAX opening.[[197]](#cite_note-DeadlineIntWknd2-199) More markets saw their gross for *Vol. 2* surpass the total gross from the first film in its third weekend,[[225]](#cite_note-DeadlineIntWknd3-227) with China following in its fourth.[[226]](#cite_note-DeadlineIntWknd4-228) The next weekend saw *Vol. 2*'s gross outside the United States and Canada ($451.1 million) surpass the international gross of the first film ($440 million).[[210]](#cite_note-DeadlineIntWknd5-212) *Vol. 2*'s three biggest markets in total earnings were: China ($99.3 million), the United Kingdom ($51.3 million), and Germany ($28 million).[[211]](#cite_note-DeadlineIntWknd6-213)

### Critical response

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Guardians_of_the_Galaxy_Vol._2&action=edit&section=17 "Edit section: Critical response")]

The [review aggregator](/wiki/Review_aggregator "Review aggregator") [Rotten Tomatoes](/wiki/Rotten_Tomatoes "Rotten Tomatoes") reported an approval rating of 85%, with an average score of 7.3/10, based on 425 reviews. The website's critical consensus reads, "*Guardians of the Galaxy Vol. 2*'s action-packed plot, dazzling visuals, and irreverent humor add up to a sequel that's almost as fun—if not quite as thrillingly fresh—as its predecessor."[[227]](#cite_note-229) On [Metacritic](/wiki/Metacritic "Metacritic"), the film has a [weighted average score](/wiki/Weighted_arithmetic_mean "Weighted arithmetic mean") of 67 out of 100, based on 47 critics, indicating "generally favorable" reviews.[[228]](#cite_note-230) Audiences polled by [CinemaScore](/wiki/CinemaScore "CinemaScore") gave the film an average grade of "A" on an A+ to F scale, while [PostTrak](/wiki/PostTrak "PostTrak") reported filmgoers gave it a 93% overall positive score and a 77% "definite recommend".[[195]](#cite_note-DeadlineWknd1-197)

[Owen Gleiberman](/wiki/Owen_Gleiberman "Owen Gleiberman") of *[Variety](/wiki/Variety_(magazine) "Variety (magazine)")* called the film "an extravagant and witty follow-up, made with the same friendly virtuosic dazzle... and just obligatory enough to be too much of a good thing." He cautioned that "this time you can sense just how hard [Gunn] is working to entertain you. Maybe a little too hard."[[229]](#cite_note-VarietyReview-231) Writing for *[Rolling Stone](/wiki/Rolling_Stone "Rolling Stone")*, [Peter Travers](/wiki/Peter_Travers "Peter Travers") described the film as a "blast" and gave it three stars out of four, praising the film for its tone and fun, soundtrack, and characters. He noted that "*Vol. 2* can't match the sneak-attack surprise of its predecessor...[but] the followup, while taking on some CGI bloat and sequel slickness, hasn't lost its love for inspired lunacy.[[230]](#cite_note-RSReview-232) *[Chicago Sun-Times](/wiki/Chicago_Sun-Times "Chicago Sun-Times")*' [Richard Roeper](/wiki/Richard_Roeper "Richard Roeper") also gave the film three stars, calling it not "quite as much fun, not quite as clever, not quite as fresh as the original—but it still packs a bright and shiny and sweet punch." Roeper continued that "even with all the silliness and all the snarkiness, the Guardians can put a lump in your throat", and praised the cast, especially Rooker, with "one of the best roles in the movie".[[231]](#cite_note-ChicagoReview-233) In his review for *[RogerEbert.com](/wiki/RogerEbert.com "RogerEbert.com")*, Brian Tallerico gave the film three stars out of four, describing it as "a thoroughly enjoyable summer blockbuster" that does not take itself seriously, avoiding "many of the flaws of the first movie, and [doing] several things notably better. It's fun, clever and a great kick-off to the summer movie season."[[232]](#cite_note-RogerEbertReview-234)

At *[The Washington Post](/wiki/The_Washington_Post "The Washington Post")*, Michael O'Sullivan gave the film four stars, praising it as being "funnier, nuttier, and more touching" than the first film. O'Sullivan felt it avoided usual sequel problems by building on the original film rather than repeating it, and also noted the use of music as well as "dazzling" visuals throughout, describing the film as "a toe-tapping, eye-popping indication that summer is here, and that it might not be so bad after all."[[233]](#cite_note-WPReview-235) Brian Lowry, writing for *[CNN.com](/wiki/CNN.com "CNN.com")*, criticized the middle portion of the film and its villain, but felt the film fared better than other Marvel sequels with its strong beginning and end, and "good-natured energy", saying it "ultimately shares just enough with its central quintet—rather heroically getting the job done, even if the trip from here to there can be a bit disjointed and messy."[[234]](#cite_note-CNNReview-236) For *[The Atlantic](/wiki/The_Atlantic "The Atlantic")*, Christopher Orr felt the film did not live up to the original, particularly due to its heavier themes and feeling that Russell was "badly miscast", but the rest of the cast, soundtrack, and humor were enough for him to give an overall positive review.[[235]](#cite_note-AtlanticReview-237)

At *The Hollywood Reporter*, [Todd McCarthy](/wiki/Todd_McCarthy "Todd McCarthy") said "*Guardians of the Galaxy Vol. 2* plays like a second ride on a roller-coaster that was a real kick the first time around but feels very been-there/done-that now."[[236]](#cite_note-THRReview-238) [Kenneth Turan](/wiki/Kenneth_Turan "Kenneth Turan") of the *[Los Angeles Times](/wiki/Los_Angeles_Times "Los Angeles Times")* was positive of the film's soundtrack and cast, especially Russell, but felt Gunn was trying too hard to re-capture the magic of the first film, and the increased scope of effects and action becomes weary. Turan concluded, "There are enough reminders of the first *Guardians* to make the sequel an acceptable experience, [but it's] less like itself and more like a standard Marvel production."[[237]](#cite_note-LAReview-239) [Manohla Dargis](/wiki/Manohla_Dargis "Manohla Dargis") at *[The New York Times](/wiki/The_New_York_Times "The New York Times")* said the film "certainly has its attractions, but most of them are visual rather than narrative." She also felt Gunn was trying too hard, and found many elements of the sequel to be too serious even with Russell balancing that with a much-needed "unforced looseness".[[238]](#cite_note-NYTReview-240) Anthony Lane in his review for *[The New Yorker](/wiki/The_New_Yorker "The New Yorker")* felt once Ego was introduced, the film began to suffer from "the curse of the backstory" and that the "point that the movie, which has been motoring along nicely, fuelled by silliness and pep, begins to splutter" was when Ego's desire for larger meaning is revealed. Lane concluded, "Let's hope that *Vol. 3* recaptures the fizz of the original, instead of slumping into the most expensive group-therapy session in the universe."[[239]](#cite_note-NewYorkerReview-241)

## Future

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Guardians_of_the_Galaxy_Vol._2&action=edit&section=19 "Edit section: Future")]

### Sequel

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Guardians_of_the_Galaxy_Vol._2&action=edit&section=20 "Edit section: Sequel")]

Main article: [Guardians of the Galaxy Vol. 3](/wiki/Guardians_of_the_Galaxy_Vol._3 "Guardians of the Galaxy Vol. 3")

*Guardians of the Galaxy Vol. 3* was released on May 5, 2023,[[258]](#cite_note-Vol3ReleaseDate-260) with Gunn returning to write and direct.[[259]](#cite_note-GunnVol3Confirm-261)[[260]](#cite_note-GunnRehired-262) Pratt, Saldaña, Bautista, Diesel, Cooper,[[261]](#cite_note-Vol3MainCast-263) Gillan,[[262]](#cite_note-GillanVol3-264) Klementieff,[[263]](#cite_note-KlementieffVol3-265) and Sean Gunn reprise their roles in the film,[[264]](#cite_note-SeanGunnVol3-266) and are joined by [Will Poulter](/wiki/Will_Poulter "Will Poulter") as [Adam Warlock](/wiki/Adam_Warlock_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Adam Warlock (Marvel Cinematic Universe)").[[6]](#cite_note-Poulter-6)

### *I Am Groot*

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Guardians_of_the_Galaxy_Vol._2&action=edit&section=21 "Edit section: I Am Groot")]

Main article: [I Am Groot](/wiki/I_Am_Groot "I Am Groot")

In December 2020, *I Am Groot*, a series of animated short films focused on Baby Groot, was announced for [Disney+](/wiki/Disney%2B "Disney+").[[265]](#cite_note-2023Vol3Release-267) Diesel reprises his role, with all five shorts released on August 10, 2022.[[266]](#cite_note-IAmGrootRelease-268)

### *The Guardians of the Galaxy Holiday Special*

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Guardians_of_the_Galaxy_Vol._2&action=edit&section=22 "Edit section: The Guardians of the Galaxy Holiday Special")]

Main article: [*The Guardians of the Galaxy Holiday Special*](/wiki/The_Guardians_of_the_Galaxy_Holiday_Special "The Guardians of the Galaxy Holiday Special")

*The Guardians of the Galaxy Holiday Special* is a [Marvel Studios Special Presentation](/wiki/Marvel_Studios_Special_Presentation "Marvel Studios Special Presentation") that was released on Disney+ on November 25, 2022,[[267]](#cite_note-HSMSSP-269)[[268]](#cite_note-HSRelease-270) with Gunn writing and directing.[[265]](#cite_note-2023Vol3Release-267) Pratt, Bautista, Diesel, Cooper, Gillan, Klementieff, Rooker, and Sean Gunn reprise their roles in the special,[[269]](#cite_note-HSCast-271) which is set before the events of *Vol. 3*.[[270]](#cite_note-HSTimeline-272)

One thing it won't have, though, is Josh Brolin's Thanos, the Big Bad of the MCU, who was formally introduced in the first movie. "I wouldn't look too hard in that movie for Thanos," confirms Feige. So far, though, a being so apparently powerful he could wear the Avengers' heads for hats has been curiously unthreatening, preferring to sit in space on what looks like a floating portapotty. Will that change? "Are you asking if he's going to do more than sit in a chair and smirk?" laughs Feige. "Oh, yes. The next time we see Thanos he will not be messing around. It will be, 'Oh, that's why they've been teasing this villain for five years!'""

Retrieved February 1, 2017. "Sneeper update. A couple weeks back I told you how the Marvel legal department advised against using Sneeper as an alien species in Guardians of the Galaxy because the word is "similar to the word meaning 'clitoris' in Icelandic." Let it be known that I wasn't protesting this decision, I just thought it was funny, so I shared it. But the Marvel legal folks are sweet people and, after all of the online articles about me not being able to use the term Sneeper, they came back and told me that maybe it's not that big a deal after all: Sneeper is cleared for use. So keep an eye out for at least one Sneeper somewhere in Guardians of the Galaxy Vol. 2."
